mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding labor income that supports aggregate consumption and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS monthly changes show a Rank IC below 0.02 with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 55% of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 5.0 percent represents restrictive monetary policy that raises corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates above the growth threshold, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal a synchronized contraction in residential investment and labor income that foreshadows falling aggregate demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in HOUST and PAYEMS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, month-over-month industrial production growth (INDPRO) falling below its trailing twelve-month trend signals capacity constraints and margin compression that reduce expected earnings quality, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO trend deviations are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of late-cycle observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures real-time consumer spending momentum that translates into near-term corporate revenue surprises and positive earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly changes show a correlation with 5-day forward returns below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS above 5 percent) and rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL monthly increase) creates a restrictive real monetary stance that compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to add predictive power (incremental R-squared < 0.01) in a 3-year rolling regression for 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market slack that precedes consumer spending and earnings contractions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases exceeding 0.2pp in 3 months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) during a monetary tightening cycle confirms a contracting output gap that amplifies downside risk to corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 prints during FEDFUNDS above 5% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.
